developer.comment: Built by Cognition (Cognition AI / Cognition Labs).

first-release.comment: Publicly announced March 2024; reached general availability Dec 10, 2024; Devin 2.0 launched Apr 3, 2025. First-release date taken from the original public announcement.

status.comment: Generally available since Dec 2024; actively developed (Devin 2.0, Sonnet 4.5 rebuild). Checked 2026-06-02.

license.comment: Closed-source commercial cloud product. Freemium: a limited free plan plus paid tiers.

specialization.comment: Broad autonomous software-engineering agent (refactors, bug fixes, feature work) — not niche-positioned.

interface.comment: Primary interface is the cloud web app (app.devin.ai); Devin 2.0 adds an agent-native cloud IDE; a VS Code extension is also offered. Also driven via Slack/Linear/MCP.

host-ides.comment: Runs in the browser (cloud IDE/app); a VS Code (and forks) extension exists to point Devin at code. Each Devin runs in its own cloud sandbox VM.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Fundamentally a cloud service; each Devin runs in a sandboxed cloud VM. Ships a VS Code extension as a secondary access point but the product shape is cloud-service.

default-model-provider.comment: Devin was rebuilt for Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 4.5; Anthropic is the underlying default model provider. Model is not user-selectable. Checked 2026-06-02.

model-choice.comment: Cognition manages the underlying model; users do not pick a model/provider within Devin.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Billed via ACU/subscription on Cognition's managed infrastructure; no user-supplied API key for the agent's model.

local-model-support.comment: Cloud-only managed service; no local/self-hosted model option.

multi-file-editing.comment: Plans and applies coordinated changes across a codebase (refactors, features) and opens PRs.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Core design: autonomous planning and execution with dynamic re-planning; chains edit/run/test/fix toward a goal with minimal per-step prompting.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs in a sandboxed VM with full shell/terminal access to build, run, and test.

mcp-support.comment: Documented MCP support both as a client (MCP Marketplace to add MCP servers) and as a server (mcp.devin.ai).

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Devin 2.0 lets users spin up multiple parallel Devins, each with its own cloud IDE — fan-out of parallel workers.

codebase-indexing.comment: Devin Wiki/Search build an index of the repo for retrieval with cited code answers and architecture diagrams.

planning-mode.comment: Devin 2.0 has an interactive planning phase — it researches the codebase and produces a detailed plan the user can refine before execution.

multimodal-input.comment: Built on Claude (vision-capable) and accepts images/screenshots as input; verified via Cognition's Claude integration. Treat as well-supported but vendor docs are sparse on the specific UI.

browser-computer-use.comment: Devin's sandbox includes a real browser it can drive (read docs, look up errors, interact with web apps) to act and verify.

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: Model-driven delegation: 'the agent may decide to delegate on its own when it determines a task would benefit from independent work', beyond explicit user invocation. A coordinator Devin autonomously breaks a task into scoped pieces and assigns each to a managed Devin. Checked 2026-06-14.

operating-mode.comment: Primarily autonomous + async/background (dispatch via Slack/Linear/UI, returns a PR). Devin 2.0's interactive IDE/planning also supports supervised collaboration.

inline-completion.comment: Task-oriented autonomous agent, not an as-you-type tab-completion tool.

background-async-execution.comment: Dispatch a task via Slack/Linear/UI and walk away; Devin messages you when the PR is ready. Core async cloud-agent behavior.

persistent-memory.comment: Knowledge base and repo Wiki persist learned context/preferences across sessions per project.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: REFRESH 2026-06-02: corrected from null to true. Devin's timeline supports a 'restore checkpoint' control that rolls back to a previous point in a session, restoring both files and memory state; environment/machine snapshots also have a Version History with restore. This is a built-in run-rollback mechanism, not just git.

git-integration.comment: Native git/GitHub awareness — branches, commits, and responds to GitHub PR comments.

pr-creation.comment: Generates first-draft pull requests and updates them in response to PR comments.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Supports project-level Knowledge and Playbooks as persistent instructions/context for the agent.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensible via MCP (client and server, MCP Marketplace), an API, and Playbooks/integrations (Slack, Linear).

permission-model.comment: Runs autonomously in a sandboxed VM but output is gated through PR review; repo/tool access is scoped and configurable (per-repo, per-tool, least-privilege). Treated as configurable with an approval gate at the PR boundary.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Enterprise offers VPC deployment — Devin's dev boxes run inside the customer's own VPC across major clouds. Not a fully on-prem install, but customer-controlled infrastructure.

privacy-mode.comment: By default Cognition does not use customer data for model training unless explicitly opted in; Enterprise customers are never trained on.

enterprise-controls.comment: SAML/OIDC SSO + SCIM, exportable session audit transcripts (SIEM), role-based access control, and VPC deployment. SOC 2 Type II.

pricing-model.comment: Free tier (limited), paid subscriptions (Pro $20, Max $200, Teams $80/seat), plus pay-as-you-go ACU overages. Checked 2026-06-02.

free-tier.comment: Free plan with limited Devin usage, Devin Review, and DeepWiki.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Cheapest paid plan is Pro at $20/mo (includes a usage quota plus pay-as-you-go overages). A limited free tier exists but the free usage is capped, so entry price reflects the cheapest paid tier. Checked 2026-06-02.

maturity.comment: GA since Dec 2024, two major versions, enterprise adoption and SOC 2 — stable and widely deployed, though the autonomous-agent UX is still evolving. Not a polished 5.

community-size.comment: High-profile and well-funded with significant enterprise traction, but as a paid cloud-only agent its hands-on user base is smaller than free/open IDE tools. Assessed medium. Checked 2026-06-02.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: MCP Marketplace plus integrations (Slack, Linear, GitHub) and an API constitute a meaningful third-party ecosystem.

project-memory-files.comment: Devin auto-loads an AGENTS.md file from the repo ('put an AGENTS.md file in your project root (or anywhere else); Devin will look for the file before it starts coding'). No CLAUDE.md/.cursorrules convention; Knowledge and Playbooks are stored in Devin's library rather than as repo-resident always-on files. Checked 2026-06-02.

memory-scopes.comment: Knowledge has enterprise-level (across all orgs) and organization-level scope, and can be pinned to a specific repo or all repos (project). AGENTS.md can be placed in the project root or any subdirectory (folder). Org-level mapped to enterprise; per-user enable/disable exists but is not a separate authoring scope. Checked 2026-06-02.

scoped-rules-files.comment: AGENTS.md is always-on (loaded before coding). Knowledge items use 'Trigger Descriptions' so Devin pulls relevant items in on demand based on context (agent-requested). No documented path/glob- or file-type-based rule activation. Checked 2026-06-02.

auto-memory.comment: Devin automatically suggests Knowledge to remember based on chat feedback and recalls it across future sessions; this is suggest-then-confirm rather than silent autonomous writing — users can edit, regenerate, or dismiss suggestions, and each item can be enabled/disabled per user. Checked 2026-06-02.

skill-support.comment: Supports Skills (SKILL.md files following the open Agent Skills standard) plus Playbooks — reusable, on-demand procedures loaded when relevant, beyond a single instruction file. Checked 2026-06-02.

skill-invocation.comment: Users invoke skills explicitly via @skills:skill-name (optionally with args); Devin also auto-activates relevant skills (model-callable). Authors can set triggers: ["user"] to disable auto-invocation. Checked 2026-06-02.

always-on-surface.comment: Progressive disclosure: only the skill name and description appear in the session's skill list by default; the full SKILL.md body is loaded and injected as a system-level instruction only when the skill is invoked. Checked 2026-06-02.

skill-distribution.comment: Git-based: skills are committed to repositories at .agents/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md (or .github/skills/, .claude/skills/) and auto-discovered/indexed across connected repos. No marketplace or package registry. Checked 2026-06-02.

sandboxing-technology.comment: Cloud Devin runs in 'Devbox', a dedicated cloud VM loaded from a machine snapshot — each session runs on its own isolated machine, with a single-tenant VPC option for Dedicated deployments (vm). The local Devin CLI's --sandbox flag 'enforces the active Read and Write permission scopes at the OS level' (os-sandbox); the docs do not name the kernel mechanism. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: Devin CLI permissions use allow/deny/ask arrays with patterns: Exec(git), Exec(npm run) (command-prefix / per-tool), Read(**) and Write(.env*) (per-path with read/write distinction), plus sandbox domain filtering (per-domain network). Assessed from the CLI rule engine. Checked 2026-06-12.

rule-precedence.comment: CLI permission engine checks deny first, then ask, then allow: 'Because deny is checked before ask, and ask is checked before allow, a deny rule always wins.' Deny-overrides; no documented specificity-based re-override or user-ordered evaluation. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-configurability.comment: Beyond user-authored declarative allow/deny rules, the CLI config supports lifecycle 'hooks' for programmable permission logic, the highest rung. Cloud Devin also exposes an Agency on/off setting (presets); CLI hooks set the ceiling. Checked 2026-06-12.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: No built-in default deny for .env/secrets. The CLI documents how an admin *can* author deny: ['Write(.env*)'], but it is not shipped enabled; repo-setup advises gitignoring .envrc so Devin doesn't commit it. Secrets are instead supplied via a Secrets dashboard. Capability exists but no default protection, so false. Checked 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: When the agent asks for permission, you can 'save the decision to your project or user config for next time' — written to .devin/config.json, .devin/config.local.json, or ~/.config/devin/config.json, surviving restarts and shared across sessions. Checked 2026-06-12.

unsupervised-mode.comment: CLI Bypass mode (aliases /yolo, /dangerous; --permission-mode dangerous) auto-approves all permission prompts including destructive commands; an Autonomous mode pairs auto-approval with the --sandbox. Cloud Devin's Agency setting likewise proceeds without per-step approval. Checked 2026-06-12.

network-access-control.comment: CLI sandbox enforces 'what they can read, write, and reach over the network' with configurable 'domain filtering' (domain-allowlist). Cloud Devbox defaults to broad internet egress (egress access required / strongly recommended) but enterprise deployments support IP allowlisting and VPC/PrivateLink scoping (configurable). Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: Cloud Devin is autonomous-by-design — with Agency on it proceeds without waiting for approval, returning a PR. The CLI's default is Normal mode, described as 'full autonomy for complex coding tasks' with the agent reading, writing and executing under normal checks (prompts only on unmatched actions, not edit-by-edit). The unconfigured first-run experience is effectively auto-run. Checked 2026-06-12.
